Defines memory regions for firmware scratch and safe firmware storage.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines memory regions for firmware storage in a system. It uses preprocessor directives to define constants that specify the start and end addresses of different firmware regions in flash memory. The `FLASH_REGION_FIRMWARE_SCRATCH_BEGIN` and `FLASH_REGION_FIRMWARE_SCRATCH_END` constants define a scratch space for firmware images, which is assumed to be 64k aligned and spans 1024k. The `FLASH_REGION_SAFE_FIRMWARE_BEGIN` and `FLASH_REGION_SAFE_FIRMWARE_END` constants define a safe firmware region, spanning 512k. The `#pragma once` directive ensures the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
